title: Sun Temple
tagline: Sun Temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Sun Temple of Modhera is a Hindu temple dedicated to the solar deity Surya, located in the village of Modhera in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: The Sun Temple of Modhera is a Hindu temple dedicated to the solar deity Surya, located in the village of Modhera in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India. The temple is situated on the bank of the river Pushpavati, and was constructed in the 11th century during the reign of Bhima I and Karna I of the Chaulukya dynasty. The temple is no longer used for worship and is a protected monument maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India. The temple complex has three components: Gūḍhamanḍapa, the shrine hall; Sabhamanḍapa, the assembly hall and Kunḍa, the reservoir.

topPlaces.name: Modhera
topPlaces.description: Modhera is a village in Becharaji Taluka in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India. The town is well known for the Sun Temple of Chaulukya era.

topPlaces.name: Modhera Vav
topPlaces.description: Modhera Vav is a stepwell in Modhera village in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India. The stepwell belongs to the 11th century while the mandapa origins from the 10th century.

topPlaces.name: Modheshwari Temple, Modhera
topPlaces.description: Modheshwari Mata Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Modhera village of Mehsana district in Gujarat, India. The temple is dedicated to the Hindu mother-goddess Modheshwari Mata, an aspect of the divine feminine Shakti.

topPlaces.name: Gambhu
topPlaces.description: Gambhu is a village in Bahucharaji Taluka of Mehsana district in Gujarat, India.

items.note: Modhera is a village in Becharaji Taluka in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India. The town is well known for the Sun Temple of Chaulukya era.
Modhera Vav is a stepwell in Modhera village in Mehsana district, Gujarat, India. The stepwell belongs to the 11th century while the mandapa origins from the 10th century.

Modheshwari Mata Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Modhera village of Mehsana district in Gujarat, India. The temple is dedicated to the Hindu mother-goddess Modheshwari Mata, an aspect of the divine feminine Shakti.
Gambhu is a village in Bahucharaji Taluka of Mehsana district in Gujarat, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sun Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sun Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sun Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (78 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sun Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sun Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sun Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sun Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sun Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Modhera, Modhera Vav, Modheshwari Temple, Modhera, Gambhu.
